buggy_code:
import pandas as pd

prices = pd.read_csv('prices.csv', index_col='date', parse_dates=True)

# Rolling with no min_periods — first values use fewer than window observations
volatility = prices['close'].pct_change().rolling(window=20).std()

# Use volatility as feature immediately without checking warmup period
features = pd.DataFrame({'vol': volatility})
features = features.dropna()
signal = (features['vol'] < 0.01).astype(int)
clean_code:
import pandas as pd

prices = pd.read_csv('prices.csv', index_col='date', parse_dates=True)

# Require full window before producing values
volatility = prices['close'].pct_change().rolling(window=20, min_periods=20).std()

features = pd.DataFrame({'vol': volatility})
features = features.dropna()
signal = (features['vol'] < 0.01).astype(int)
description: Rolling window without min_periods produces values from fewer observations than the window size.
explanation: Without min_periods=window, pandas returns partial window results for early rows. A 20-day volatility computed from 3 days of data is misleading and creates artificially low early volatility readings that can generate false signals.
suggested_fix: Set min_periods equal to window size to only produce values when a full window is available.

issues.title: Rolling window without min_periods uses partial data
Rolling window without min_periods uses partial data
